title: Epic: Orchestrator Core Reliability

description:
TASK CONTEXT
- Bead ID: beadboard-0cf.1
- Title: Replace event-based chat with real conversation turns
- Parent/Epic: beadboard-0cf (Orchestrator Core Reliability)
- Dependencies: none

TASK CONTRACT
- Goal: Replace the string-matching event reconstruction in orchestrator-chat.ts with first-class conversation turn storage (user message, assistant response) so the chat transcript is reliable.
- Success Criteria:
  - Orchestrator chat stores actual conversation turns, not reconstructed from event title matching
  - No double-reply rendering (fixes beadboard-sen)
  - No 40-event hard cap silently dropping older messages
  - No string-matching on "Orchestrator Reply" / "Orchestrator Thinking" titles
  - Existing SSE stream still works for runtime console events
- Scope:
  - orchestrator-chat.ts — rewrite projection logic
  - pi-daemon-adapter.ts — emit conversation turns instead of/alongside events
  - orchestrator-panel.tsx — consume new turn model
- Out of Scope:
  - Persistence to disk (separate bead beadboard-0cf.2)
  - Worker communication display
  - Runtime console changes

VERIFICATION REQUIREMENTS
- npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npm run test
- Manual test: send 3+ prompts to orchestrator, verify no duplicate replies, verify scrollback works beyond 40 messages
title: Replace event-based chat with real conversation turns

description:
TASK CONTEXT
- Bead ID: beadboard-0cf.2
- Parent/Epic: beadboard-0cf
- Dependencies: none

TASK CONTRACT
- Goal: Persist orchestrator state (events, workers, conversation turns) to disk so it survives page refresh and dev server restart.
- Success Criteria:
  - Events written to .beadboard/runtime/events.jsonl on every state change
  - Worker state written to .beadboard/runtime/workers.jsonl on spawn/complete/fail
  - On server startup, state is restored from disk files
  - User can refresh the page and see the same orchestrator state
- Scope:
  - embedded-daemon.ts — add write-through to disk
  - worker-session-manager.ts — persist worker state
  - API routes — read persisted state on init
- Out of Scope:
  - Daemon architecture (separate process)
  - Cross-session context for the LLM itself

VERIFICATION REQUIREMENTS
- npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npm run test
- Manual test: start orchestrator, spawn worker, refresh page, verify state restored
title: Persist orchestrator state to disk

description:
TASK CONTEXT
- Bead ID: beadboard-0cf.3
- Parent/Epic: beadboard-0cf
- Dependencies: none

TASK CONTRACT
- Goal: Replace all execFileSync('bd', ...) calls in TUI tool handlers with async execFile to prevent blocking the Node.js event loop.
- Success Criteria:
  - bb-spawn-worker.ts uses async subprocess
  - bb-bead-crud.ts uses async subprocess
  - bb-worker-results.ts uses async subprocess
  - A hanging bd command no longer freezes the entire server
- Scope:
  - src/tui/tools/bb-spawn-worker.ts
  - src/tui/tools/bb-bead-crud.ts
  - src/tui/tools/bb-worker-results.ts
  - Any other tool files using execFileSync
- Out of Scope:
  - Changing tool interfaces or return types
  - Adding timeout handling (separate concern)

VERIFICATION REQUIREMENTS
- npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npm run test
- grep -r "execFileSync" src/tui/ returns zero results
title: Replace execFileSync with async in tool handlers

description:
TASK CONTEXT
- Bead ID: beadboard-0cf.4
- Parent/Epic: beadboard-0cf
- Dependencies: none

TASK CONTRACT
- Goal: Add context window management so the orchestrator system prompt doesn't consume the entire context on projects with many beads.
- Success Criteria:
  - System prompt filters or summarizes active tasks instead of including all as JSON
  - Configurable max beads injected (default ~20 most relevant)
  - Relevance sorting: prioritize in_progress, blocked, recently updated
  - Orchestrator can still request full task list via bb_dolt_read tool when needed
- Scope:
  - src/tui/system-prompt.ts — add filtering/truncation
- Out of Scope:
  - LLM session rotation or context windowing
  - Changing the Pi SDK session management

VERIFICATION REQUIREMENTS
- npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npm run test
- Test with 50+ beads: system prompt stays under 4000 tokens
title: Add context window management for orchestrator

description:
TASK CONTEXT
- Bead ID: beadboard-0cf.5
- Parent/Epic: beadboard-0cf
- Dependencies: beadboard-0cf.1, beadboard-0cf.2

TASK CONTRACT
- Goal: Write integration tests for the orchestrator critical path to prove it works under real conditions.
- Success Criteria:
  - Tests cover: session creation, prompt flow, worker spawn, worker completion, worker failure
  - Tests run as part of npm run test
  - Tests use mocked Pi SDK (not real API calls)
  - At least 5 test cases covering the happy path and 2 error cases
- Scope:
  - tests/lib/orchestrator-*.test.ts (new files)
  - tests/lib/worker-session.test.ts (new file)
  - Register in package.json test script
- Out of Scope:
  - E2E browser tests
  - Testing the Pi SDK itself
  - Performance/load testing

VERIFICATION REQUIREMENTS
- npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npm run test (all pass including new tests)
title: Add orchestrator integration tests

description: Agent screwed up? Roll back to the Dolt commit before the session started. Undo everything it did to the bead state in one action.
title: Add rollback of agent work via Dolt

description: Every agent action is a Dolt mutation. Diff the state before and after an agent session to see exactly what it did to the beads database. Surface this as a 'session diff' view.
title: Add session replay via Dolt diffs
Epic: Orchestrator UX

description:
TASK CONTEXT
- Bead ID: beadboard-1zb.2
- Parent/Epic: beadboard-1zb
- Dependencies: none

TASK CONTRACT
- Goal: Surface BLOCKED agent messages as a visible notification the user can't miss, even when on another view.
- Success Criteria:
  - BLOCKED messages from agents trigger a visible indicator in the top bar or orchestrator tab
  - Indicator persists until the user opens the orchestrator and sees the message
  - Works across all views (social, graph, activity)
  - Clicking the indicator navigates to the orchestrator chat

  - Sound alerts

VERIFICATION REQUIREMENTS

description:
TASK CONTEXT
- Bead ID: beadboard-1zb.3
- Parent/Epic: beadboard-1zb
- Dependencies: none

TASK CONTRACT
- Goal: Add stop worker and restart orchestrator controls to the UI.
- Success Criteria:
  - Each active worker has a "Stop" button in the runtime console or agent status panel
  - Clicking stop calls workerSessionManager.terminateWorker() and updates UI
  - Orchestrator has a "Restart" button that kills the session and creates a fresh one
  - Stopped workers update their bead status appropriately
- Scope:
  - runtime-console.tsx or agent-status-panel.tsx — stop buttons
  - orchestrator-panel.tsx — restart button
  - Worker session termination API
- Out of Scope:
  - Graceful shutdown negotiation with workers
  - Auto-restart on failure

VERIFICATION REQUIREMENTS
- npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npm run test
- Manual test: spawn worker, click stop, verify it terminates and bead updates
title: Add stop worker and restart orchestrator buttons

description:
TASK CONTEXT
- Bead ID: beadboard-1zb.4
- Parent/Epic: beadboard-1zb
- Dependencies: none

TASK CONTRACT
- Goal: Show the template pipeline when orchestrator uses a template — which agents are planned, which is active, what's next.
- Success Criteria:
  - When a template is selected, show a pipeline visualization: e.g., Architect → Engineer x2 → Reviewer → Tester
  - Active step highlighted, completed steps checked, pending steps dimmed

  - Read template definition to build pipeline stages
- Out of Scope:
  - Editing the pipeline mid-flight (separate bead)
  - Custom template creation UI

VERIFICATION REQUIREMENTS
- npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npm run test
title: Add plan visibility — show template pipeline

description:
TASK CONTEXT
- Bead ID: beadboard-1zb.5
- Parent/Epic: beadboard-1zb

TASK CONTRACT

- Success Criteria:

VERIFICATION REQUIREMENTS

title: Add launch-from-graph entry point

description:
TASK CONTEXT
- Bead ID: beadboard-1zb.6
- Parent/Epic: beadboard-1zb
- Dependencies: none

TASK CONTRACT
- Goal: Add retry mechanism for failed workers.
- Success Criteria:
  - Failed workers show a "Retry" button in the UI
  - Clicking retry spawns a fresh worker on the same bead with same agent type
  - Failed worker's session is cleaned up
  - Bead status reset to open before retry
- Scope:
  - runtime-console.tsx or agent-status-panel.tsx — retry button
  - worker-session-manager.ts — retry logic
  - API endpoint for retry
- Out of Scope:
  - Auto-retry policies
  - Retry with different agent type

VERIFICATION REQUIREMENTS
- npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npm run test
title: Add retry failed workers

description:
TASK CONTEXT
- Bead ID: beadboard-1zb.7
- Parent/Epic: beadboard-1zb
- Dependencies: none

TASK CONTRACT
- Goal: Add expandable/fullscreen mode for the orchestrator chat panel.
- Success Criteria:

  - Keyboard shortcut to toggle (e.g., Cmd+Shift+O)
  - Collapsed state returns to normal sidebar width

description:
TASK CONTEXT
- Bead ID: beadboard-1zb.8
- Parent/Epic: beadboard-1zb
- Dependencies: none

TASK CONTRACT
- Goal: Add orchestrator settings surface for model, API key, max workers, preferences.
- Success Criteria:
  - Settings panel or page accessible from orchestrator UI
  - Configurable: model selection, API key, max concurrent workers
  - Settings persisted to .beadboard/config.json
  - Changes take effect on next orchestrator session (no hot-reload needed)
- Scope:
  - New settings component or panel
  - .beadboard/config.json read/write
  - Pi SDK session creation reads config
- Out of Scope:
  - Per-project settings overrides
  - Template management UI

VERIFICATION REQUIREMENTS
- npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npm run test
title: Add orchestrator settings surface

description: orchestrator-chat.ts reconstructs conversation by string-matching event titles. Replace with first-class conversation turn storage. Fixes double-reply (beadboard-sen), 40-event cap, and string-matching fragility.
title: Replace event-based chat with real conversation turns

description: EmbeddedPiDaemon stores RuntimeConsoleEvents in an array per project with no eviction policy. A long-running server accumulates events without bound, leading to memory growth. Add a cap (e.g. 1000 events) with oldest-first eviction.
title: Bug: unbounded event array in embedded-daemon

description: When a worker fails, the bead is still open but there's no way to retry. Add retry button or command that spawns a fresh worker on the same bead.
title: Add retry failed workers

description: getOrCreateSession in pi-daemon-adapter.ts is not async-safe. If two prompts arrive before the first Pi SDK session resolves, both hit the creation branch and two sessions get created. The second overwrites the first in activeSessions map, leaking the first session. Fix: add a session creation lock/promise cache per project root.
title: Bug: session race condition in pi-daemon-adapter

description: System prompt includes ALL active tasks as JSON. On a project with 50+ beads this eats the context window. Need truncation strategy, relevance filtering, or summarization.
title: Add context window management for orchestrator

description: bb-spawn-worker.ts, bb-bead-crud.ts, bb-worker-results.ts use execFileSync('bd', ...) which blocks the Node.js event loop. Replace with execFile (async). If bd hangs, the entire server currently freezes.
title: Replace execFileSync with async subprocess calls
Epic: Dolt History Integration

description:
TASK CONTEXT
- Bead ID: beadboard-6rp.1
- Parent/Epic: beadboard-6rp
- Dependencies: none

TASK CONTRACT
- Goal: Surface session diffs from Dolt — show exactly what an agent session changed in the beads database.
- Success Criteria:
  - After a worker completes, can view a Dolt diff of bead state before/after the session
  - Diff shows: beads created, beads updated (with field-level changes), beads closed
  - Accessible from the agent status panel or orchestrator chat
- Scope:
  - New util: dolt-diff.ts — query bd dolt diff for a commit range
  - Agent status panel or review component — render the diff
- Out of Scope:
  - File-level diffs (code changes, not bead changes)
  - Interactive diff editing

VERIFICATION REQUIREMENTS
- npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npm run test
title: Add session replay via Dolt diffs

description:
TASK CONTEXT
- Bead ID: beadboard-6rp.2
- Parent/Epic: beadboard-6rp
- Dependencies: beadboard-6rp.1

TASK CONTRACT
- Goal: Add ability to roll back agent work by reverting to the Dolt commit before the session started.
- Success Criteria:
  - "Rollback" button on completed/failed worker sessions
  - Clicking rolls back to the Dolt commit hash recorded at session start
  - Beads return to pre-session state
  - Confirmation dialog before rollback
- Scope:
  - Record Dolt commit hash at session start in worker state
  - Rollback action calls bd dolt checkout or equivalent
  - UI button on agent status panel
- Out of Scope:
  - Partial rollback (only some beads)
  - Rollback of code changes (only bead state)

VERIFICATION REQUIREMENTS
- npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npm run test
title: Add rollback of agent work via Dolt

description:
TASK CONTEXT
- Bead ID: beadboard-6rp.3
- Parent/Epic: beadboard-6rp
- Dependencies: none

TASK CONTRACT
- Goal: Surface session history from Dolt commit log in the orchestrator UI.
- Success Criteria:
  - Orchestrator shows a history of past sessions derived from Dolt commits
  - Each entry shows: timestamp, which agent, what beads were affected, outcome
  - Clickable to expand into session detail
- Scope:
  - Query bd dolt log for orchestrator-related commits
  - New history component or section in orchestrator panel
- Out of Scope:
  - Full Dolt branch management UI
  - Cross-project history aggregation

VERIFICATION REQUIREMENTS
- npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npm run test
title: Surface session history from Dolt log

description:
TASK CONTEXT
- Bead ID: beadboard-6rp.4
- Parent/Epic: beadboard-6rp
- Dependencies: beadboard-6rp.1

TASK CONTRACT
- Goal: Add a review surface where users see what changed before trusting agent output.
- Success Criteria:
  - When an agent closes a bead, a review card appears showing: Dolt diff, evidence text, files mentioned

- Out of Scope:

  - Automated approval rules

VERIFICATION REQUIREMENTS
- npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npm run test
title: Add output review surface

description:
TASK CONTEXT
- Bead ID: beadboard-6rp.5
- Parent/Epic: beadboard-6rp
- Dependencies: none

TASK CONTRACT
- Goal: Surface Dolt version history as an audit trail in the activity view.
- Success Criteria:
  - Activity view shows Dolt commits as events: who changed what bead, when, mutation type
  - Integrates with existing activity feed alongside SSE events
  - Filterable by agent, bead, or time range

- Out of Scope:

  - Cross-project audit

VERIFICATION REQUIREMENTS

title: Add Dolt audit trail to activity view

description:
TASK CONTEXT
- Bead ID: beadboard-6rp.6
- Parent/Epic: beadboard-6rp
- Dependencies: none

TASK CONTRACT
- Goal: Show token usage and estimated cost per orchestrator session so users know what they're spending.
- Success Criteria:
  - Token counter visible in orchestrator panel header or runtime console
  - Shows total tokens used across orchestrator + all workers in this session
  - Estimated cost based on model pricing (configurable)
  - Updates in realtime as agents work

  - Aggregate across orchestrator + worker sessions

- Out of Scope:

VERIFICATION REQUIREMENTS

description: All orchestrator state (workers, events, sessions) is in-memory and dies on page refresh. Write events.jsonl + workers.jsonl to .beadboard/runtime/ on every state change. Read on startup.
title: Persist orchestrator state to disk
Epic: Agent Visibility

description:
TASK CONTEXT
- Bead ID: beadboard-7gx.1
- Parent/Epic: beadboard-7gx
- Dependencies: none

TASK CONTRACT
- Goal: Show meaningful agent activity detail — which file they're editing, what command they're running — instead of just "working."
- Success Criteria:
  - Worker events include current action context (file path, command, tool name)
  - Runtime console shows "Engineer 01: editing src/lib/parser.ts" not just "Engineer 01: working"
  - Agent status panel shows last action for each worker
  - Updates stream via SSE in near-realtime
- Scope:
  - worker-session-manager.ts — capture tool-use events from Pi SDK
  - embedded-daemon.ts — store action context on events
  - runtime-console.tsx — render action detail
  - agent-status-panel.tsx — show last action
- Out of Scope:
  - Full tool execution log (too verbose)
  - File diff preview while working

VERIFICATION REQUIREMENTS
- npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npm run test
title: Show real agent activity detail

description:
TASK CONTEXT
- Bead ID: beadboard-7gx.2
- Parent/Epic: beadboard-7gx
- Dependencies: none

TASK CONTRACT
- Goal: Show agent avatars/indicators on graph nodes they're working on so you can see work flowing through the dependency graph.
- Success Criteria:
  - Graph nodes for in_progress beads show the assigned agent's display name or avatar
  - When a worker completes and the next bead becomes unblocked, visual flows to the next node
  - Agent indicators update via SSE as assignments change
- Scope:
  - smart-dag.tsx — render agent indicators on nodes
  - Graph data model — include assignee info on nodes
- Out of Scope:
  - Animated transitions between nodes
  - Agent trail/history on graph

VERIFICATION REQUIREMENTS
- npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npm run test
title: Add agent presence on graph nodes

description:
TASK CONTEXT
- Bead ID: beadboard-7gx.3
- Parent/Epic: beadboard-7gx
- Dependencies: none

TASK CONTRACT
- Goal: Allow user to address specific workers directly, not just through the orchestrator.
- Success Criteria:
  - User can @mention a worker in orchestrator chat (e.g., "@Engineer 01 use the other approach")
  - Orchestrator relays the message to the specific worker via mailbox
  - Or: separate chat channel per worker accessible from agent status panel
- Scope:
  - orchestrator-panel.tsx — @mention parsing or worker channel selector
  - Mailbox routing for direct messages
- Out of Scope:
  - Real-time back-and-forth conversation with individual workers
  - Worker-initiated messages to user (goes through orchestrator)

VERIFICATION REQUIREMENTS
- npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npm run test
title: Add direct communication with specific workers

description: When an agent finishes, show what actually changed: Dolt diff of bead state, files modified, evidence provided. A review surface before you trust the output.
title: Add output review surface — see what changed

description: New users see an empty chat with a placeholder. Add suggested prompts like 'Work on the top priority bug', 'Show me what needs doing', 'Spawn a feature-dev team for X'. Show what the orchestrator can do.
title: Add onboarding / suggested prompts to empty orchestrator panel

description: Each worker burns API credits with no visibility. Add a token/cost counter per orchestrator session so users know what they're spending.
title: Add cost / token visibility per session

description: When orchestrator uses a template, show the pipeline: architect first, then engineers, then reviewer, then tester. Show which step is active, what's coming next, what's done.
title: Add plan visibility — show template pipeline and what's next

description: pi-runtime-detection.ts line 50 has a hardcoded fallback path '/home/clawdbot/npm-global/lib/node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent' — a WSL-specific remnant from dev. It's inert by default (allowLinkedPi=false) but should be removed before shipping to external users.
title: Cleanup: remove hardcoded clawdbot path from pi-runtime-detection

description: Launch failure, bootstrap failure on mount, and prompt fetch failures are all silently caught with empty catch blocks. User sees nothing happen when things fail. Surface errors via runtime console events or error state in the UI. Key locations: unified-shell.tsx bootstrapRuntime catch, handleAskOrchestrator catch, orchestrator-panel.tsx submit catch.
title: Bug: silent failures in orchestrator UX

description: No way to stop a stuck worker or restart a dead orchestrator from the UI. User currently has to refresh the page. Add per-worker stop button and orchestrator restart button.
title: Add stop worker and restart orchestrator buttons

description: Surface Dolt version history in the activity view. Who changed what bead, when, with what mutation. Full audit trail from data that already exists.
title: Add Dolt audit trail to activity view

description: The DAG graph shows task dependencies but not which agents are working on which nodes. Add agent avatars/indicators on graph nodes so you can SEE work flowing through the dependency graph.
title: Add agent presence on graph nodes

description: No configuration surface at all. Need: model selection, API key management, max concurrent workers, template preferences. Could be a settings panel or orchestrator commands.
title: Add orchestrator settings surface

description: Can only talk to the orchestrator. Sometimes you want to give instructions directly to Engineer 01. Add ability to address specific workers or open a direct channel.
title: Add direct communication with specific workers

description: Dolt commits ARE the session log. Surface 'yesterday at 3pm, Engineer 01 closed beadboard-sen, updated 3 beads' from bd dolt log. Gives session history for free.
title: Surface session history from Dolt log

description: pi-daemon-adapter.ts emits both text_delta and text_done as 'Orchestrator Reply' events, causing every assistant message to appear twice in the chat UI. The text_done handler re-emits the full concatenated text that was already streamed via deltas. Fix: suppress the duplicate text_done emission or deduplicate by content hash.
title: Bug: orchestrator double-reply rendering

description: The orchestrator chat is a left sidebar — too cramped for the primary interface. Add ability to expand to wider panel or fullscreen mode for complex conversations.
title: Add chat panel expand / fullscreen mode

description: When an agent sends a BLOCKED message, it shows up in the event stream but there's no notification. Need a visible indicator — badge, banner, or red highlight — that grabs attention even if user is on another view.
title: Add BLOCKED notification that can't be missed

description: Zero tests exist for any orchestrator code. Write 5-10 integration tests covering: session creation, prompt flow, worker spawn, worker completion, worker failure.
title: Add orchestrator integration tests

description: Currently shows 'Engineer 01 working' with no detail. Surface what the agent is actually doing: which file they're editing, what command they ran, meaningful progress updates instead of a black box.
title: Show real agent activity detail — files, commands, progress

description: Rocket button only exists on social cards. The graph view shows dependency structure — that's where you'd naturally want to say 'work on this subtree' or 'unblock this chain'. Add orchestrator launch from graph nodes.
title: Add launch-from-graph entry point
